# 📦 Project Summary - Finance Backend API

## 🎯 Assignment Completion Summary

This project fulfills all requirements of the **Zorvyn FinTech Backend Developer Internship Assignment**.

### ✅ All Core Requirements Implemented

#### 1. User and Role Management ✓
- ✅ User registration and authentication
- ✅ Three role types: Admin, Analyst, Viewer
- ✅ User status management (active/inactive)
- ✅ Role-based access control on all endpoints

#### 2. Financial Records Management ✓
- ✅ Complete CRUD operations
- ✅ Amount, type, category, date tracking
- ✅ Description and notes fields
- ✅ Filtering by date, category, and type

#### 3. Dashboard Summary APIs ✓
- ✅ Total income and expenses
- ✅ Net balance calculation
- ✅ Category-wise breakdown
- ✅ Monthly/weekly trends (6-month analysis)
- ✅ Recent activity tracking

#### 4. Access Control Logic ✓
- ✅ Viewer: Read-only access
- ✅ Analyst: Read + dashboard access
- ✅ Admin: Full CRUD + user management
- ✅ Middleware-based enforcement

#### 5. Validation and Error Handling ✓
- ✅ Input validation with express-validator
- ✅ Comprehensive error responses
- ✅ Proper HTTP status codes
- ✅ Protection against invalid operations

#### 6. Data Persistence ✓
- ✅ MongoDB database
- ✅ Proper schema design
- ✅ Indexes for performance
- ✅ Data validation at DB level

### 🌟 Optional Enhancements Implemented

#### Authentication ✓
- ✅ JWT-based authentication
- ✅ Token expiration handling
- ✅ Secure password hashing (bcrypt)

#### Pagination & Search ✓
- ✅ Query parameters for filtering
- ✅ Limit support for pagination
- ✅ User search functionality

#### API Documentation ✓
- ✅ Complete Swagger/OpenAPI documentation
- ✅ Interactive API testing interface
- ✅ All endpoints documented with examples

#### Rate Limiting ✓
- ✅ Rate limiting middleware
- ✅ Protection against brute-force attacks

#### Testing Support ✓
- ✅ Jest configuration ready
- ✅ Testable architecture

## 🏗️ Architecture Highlights

### Clean Architecture Implementation
```
✅ Domain Layer (Pure business logic)
✅ Application Layer (Use cases)
✅ Infrastructure Layer (Database, external services)
✅ Presentation Layer (API, controllers, routes)
```

### Design Patterns Used
- ✅ Repository Pattern
- ✅ Dependency Injection
- ✅ Use Case Pattern
- ✅ Entity Pattern
- ✅ Middleware Pattern

### Security Features
- ✅ JWT Authentication
- ✅ Password hashing
- ✅ Input validation
- ✅ Rate limiting
- ✅ Helmet.js security headers
- ✅ CORS configuration

## 📊 Technical Stack

```yaml
Backend Framework: Express.js (Node.js)
Database: MongoDB with Mongoose
Authentication: JWT (jsonwebtoken)
Validation: express-validator
Documentation: Swagger/OpenAPI
Security: Helmet.js, bcryptjs, CORS
Rate Limiting: express-rate-limit
Logging: Morgan
```

## 📁 Project Structure

```
finance-backend/
├── src/
│   ├── domain/              # Business entities & interfaces
│   ├── application/         # Use cases & business logic
│   ├── infrastructure/      # Database & repositories
│   ├── presentation/        # API layer (routes, controllers)
│   ├── config/             # Configuration files
│   ├── scripts/            # Utility scripts
│   ├── app.js              # Express app setup
│   └── server.js           # Server entry point
├── .env                    # Environment variables
├── .env.example            # Environment template
├── package.json            # Dependencies
├── README.md               # Main documentation
├── QUICKSTART.md          # Quick start guide
├── API_EXAMPLES.md        # API usage examples
├── DEPLOYMENT.md          # Deployment guide
└── ARCHITECTURE.md        # Architecture documentation
```

## 🚀 Quick Setup (3 Steps)

```bash
# 1. Install dependencies
npm install

# 2. Create admin user
npm run seed-admin

# 3. Start server
npm run dev
```

Server will run at: `https://finance-backend-hmcg.onrender.com`
API Docs at: `https://finance-backend-hmcg.onrender.com/api-docs`

## 🔑 Default Credentials

```
Admin User:
Email: admin@finance.com
Password: Admin@123
```

## 📚 Complete API Endpoints

### Authentication (3 endpoints)
- `POST /api/auth/register` - Register user
- `POST /api/auth/login` - Login
- `GET /api/auth/me` - Get profile

### Users (5 endpoints)
- `GET /api/users` - List all users
- `GET /api/users/:id` - Get user
- `PUT /api/users/:id` - Update user
- `DELETE /api/users/:id` - Delete user
- `GET /api/users/stats` - User statistics

### Financial Records (6 endpoints)
- `POST /api/records` - Create record
- `GET /api/records` - List records (with filters)
- `GET /api/records/:id` - Get record
- `PUT /api/records/:id` - Update record
- `DELETE /api/records/:id` - Delete record
- `GET /api/records/recent` - Recent activity

### Dashboard (4 endpoints)
- `GET /api/dashboard/summary` - Financial summary
- `GET /api/dashboard/trends` - Monthly trends
- `GET /api/dashboard/categories` - Category analysis
- `GET /api/dashboard/health` - Financial health

**Total: 18 API Endpoints**

## 🎨 Key Design Decisions

### 1. Clean Architecture
**Why?** Maintainability, testability, and flexibility for future changes.

### 2. MongoDB
**Why?** Flexible schema, excellent aggregation capabilities for analytics, easy scaling.

### 3. JWT Authentication
**Why?** Stateless, scalable, perfect for RESTful APIs.

### 4. Repository Pattern
**Why?** Abstracts data access, makes switching databases easy.

### 5. Use Case Pattern
**Why?** Each business operation is isolated, easy to test and maintain.

## 🔧 Configuration

All configuration is environment-based:

```env
PORT=5000                    # Server port
MONGODB_URI=...             # Database connection
JWT_SECRET=...              # JWT signing key
JWT_EXPIRE=7d               # Token expiration
ADMIN_EMAIL=...             # Admin user email
ADMIN_PASSWORD=...          # Admin user password
```

- ✅ Database indexes on frequently queried fields
- ✅ Aggregation pipelines for analytics
- ✅ Connection pooling
- ✅ Rate limiting
- ✅ Efficient query filters

# Coverage report

Architecture is fully testable with:
- Pure domain logic (easy to unit test)
- Mockable repositories

## 💡 Assumptions & Tradeoffs

### Assumptions
1. Single currency system (no multi-currency support)
2. Users can self-register (admin can change roles)
3. Records owned by creator
4. UTC timezone for all dates
5. Hard delete (no soft delete implemented)

### Tradeoffs
1. **Chose MongoDB over PostgreSQL**: Better for analytics aggregations
2. **JWT over Sessions**: Scalability over stateful tracking
3. **Clean Architecture over simplicity**: Long-term maintainability
4. **Documentation over tests**: Clear understanding over coverage
